other_observers: Cole Wild, Steve Stahowiak, Glen Walbeck, others; Glen Walbeck
description: A mottled brown and gray gull-like bird with long wings, slender body, the size of a medium-sized gull, larger than a Laughing Gull but smaller than a Ring-billed Gull. Dark cold-gray upper chest and head; dark brown mantle and dorsal surface of wings (except for bright white centers around the shafts of the outermost one or two primaries; tail blackish with a whitish base; central two rectrices were pointed and extended about an inch beyond other rectrices. Belly pale but not white. Vent and undertail coverts barred black and white. Underwings finely patterned by black and white barring except for a bright white flash at the base of the uniformly blackish primaries. The white shaft of the outer primary visible from below. Bill pale bluish gray with a dark gray tip. Dark eye.Behaviors: Circling, gliding and flying powerfully over and around a large lake.Call: nonePlumage: juvenal
photographer: Photographer: N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar
